summary: loadutils: name and README impersonate webpack loader-utils; pulls non-standard 'lessload' dependency
details: Package name 'loadutils' and README documentation (urlToRequest, interpolateName, getHashDigest) mimic the popular webpack 'loader-utils' package, but the shipped code is an unrelated debug-style logger. The contributor block reuses the name 'Kiko Beats' with a homepage URL ('alphacointech1010.com') that does not match the real maintainer's site, suggesting identity impersonation. src/index.js requires 'lessload' as its core logging primitive — a non-standard package whose name resembles a typosquat of 'debug'. loadutils itself contains no exfiltration, RCE, credential reads, or install-time hooks; any installer harm would have to arrive via the 'lessload' transitive dependency, which is not analyzed in this record. Name-similarity and identity impersonation are suspicious signals but do not by themselves demonstrate installer-side harm in this package version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
